def: part. adj. Lying concealed, hidden, invisible
subs: v.n. To become invisible, to vanish, disappear, abscond, scamper off, run away.

def: part. adj. = چمپت ćampat, q.v.
adj. & s.f. Of the colour of the ćampā flower, yellow, golden, orange-coloured; — yellow colour, &c.
adv. (old H.), Unperceived, secretly, stealthily; in secret.
s.f. A kind of elaborate and highly artificial composition in which the same subject is continued through alternations in prose and verse.
s.m. = ćappū, q.v.
s.m. = ćamṛā, q.v.
s.m. A hide, skin, leather
subs: ćamrā udheṛnā, ćamṛā ćhuṛānā, ćamṛā khaiṅćnā, ćamṛā nikālnā (-kā), To skin, to flay; — ćamṛā udheṛnā or udheṛ-denā (-kā), To skin or excoriate by beating.

def: s.f. The leathern strap which goes round a spinning-wheel; — a lean or skinny woman (cf. ćamćaṛakh).
s.m. A sore on the foot (caused by the friction of a shoe).

ety: Prk. चम्मआरअवत्ती and चम्मआरअवट्टी; S. चर्मकार+क+वार्त्ता and वृत्तिः
def: s.f. The perquisites of a ćamār.

ety: Prk. चम्मआरअवत्ती and चम्मआरअवट्टी; S. चर्मकार+क+वार्त्ता and वृत्तिः
def: s.f. The perquisites of a ćamār.
s.f. An army; division of an army; a squadron.
s.m. A body-louse, a tick (infesting cattle; — syn. ćicṛī).
s.f. A form of Durgā.
s.m. (prov.), A kind of sugar-cane.
s.m. The lower jaw; the chin; jaw, jabber, talk; a lump of dough (enough to make a cake).
s.m. The chick-pea, Cicer arietenum, commonly called 'gram'
subs: ćane-se bhūnnā or bhūn-denā, v.t. To roast or parch like gram; to destroy (as with a volley); — v.n. To be parched like gram; to be in a great state of heat, or in a fever (as the body).

def: s.m. The chick-pea ( = ćanā, q.v.).

r: ćanak, ćinak, ćankā, ćanaknā, ćik, ćanak-bāʼo
def: s.m. = ćanak-bāʼī, s.f. Rheumatic pain, rheumatism (in the back, &c.).
s.m. = ćanak, q.v.

ety: ćanak˚ = Prk. चणक्क(इ) or चणक्के(इ), fr. S. चण् (or चन्+कृ
def: v.n. To burst and fall out (as seed from the husk); to burst or open (as a bud); to crack (as earthenware); — to be irritated ( = ćinćinanā; — cf. ćaṭaknā).
s.m. Name of a class of Rājpūts.
s.m. The plane-tree; the poplar; — a circle stained (by women) on the hand or foot with privet or ḥinnā.

adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Moving to and fro, movable, trembling, shaking, tremulous; unsteady, restless, unstable, inconstant, inconsiderate, changeful, volatile, fickle, uncertain; heedless, lawless; wanton, sportive, playful; transient, perishable; — an unsteady character, an inconstant or fickle person, &c.; a libertine
subs: adj. Of uncertain disposition, capricious, fickle, false-hearted.

def: s.f. Lightning; — the goddess of fortune.

s.f. = H چنچلائي चंचलाई ćanćalāʼī, s.f. = H چنچلپن चंचलपन ćanćal-pan, s.m. = H چنچلپنا चंचलपना ćanćal-panā, s.m. Movableness, unsteadiness; restlessness, inconstancy, uncertainty, capriciousness, fickleness; wantonness, playfulness; transitoriness, perishableness.

s.f. = H چنچلاهٿ चंचलाहट ćanćalāhaṭ, s.f. = H چنچلپن चंचलपन ćanćal-pan, s.m. = H چنچلپنا चंचलपना ćanćal-panā, s.m. Movableness, unsteadiness; restlessness, inconstancy, uncertainty, capriciousness, fickleness; wantonness, playfulness; transitoriness, perishableness.
s.f. = S چنچلتو चञ्चलत्व ćanćalatva, s.m. = ćanćalāʼī, ćanćal-panā, q.v.
s.m. = S چنچلتا चञ्चलता ćanćalatā, s.f. = ćanćalāʼī, ćanćal-panā, q.v.

s.m. = H چنچلاهٿ चंचलाहट ćanćalāhaṭ, s.f. = H چنچلائي चंचलाई ćanćalāʼī, s.f. = H چنچلپنا चंचलपना ćanćal-panā, s.m. Movableness, unsteadiness; restlessness, inconstancy, uncertainty, capriciousness, fickleness; wantonness, playfulness; transitoriness, perishableness.

s.m. = H چنچلاهٿ चंचलाहट ćanćalāhaṭ, s.f. = H چنچلائي चंचलाई ćanćalāʼī, s.f. = H چنچلپن चंचलपन ćanćal-pan, s.m. Movableness, unsteadiness; restlessness, inconstancy, uncertainty, capriciousness, fickleness; wantonness, playfulness; transitoriness, perishableness.
s.m. Unsteadiness, fickleness, restlessness, mutability.
s.f. Simmering, crackling, &c.; throbbing, shooting, throb, &c.

ety: ćanćanā˚ = S. चण् redupl.+ā = āw = Prk. आवे = S. आपि caus. augment.
def: v.n. To simmer, to crackle, to sputter (as butter, &c. in a frying-pan); — to shoot, to throb, to smart, tingle.
s.m. = ćāćar, q.v.
adj. & s.m. = ćaćar, q.v.
s.m. A bee (syn. bhramar).
v.t. To scrape up, to grub (as grass, &c.).
s.f. The plant Achyranthes alternifolia.
s.f. An inferior kind of grain (as mūṅg or jawār); — the grain which remains in the ear after treading ont.
s.f. Beak, bill (of a bird = ćoṅć, q.v.).

ety: Prk. चन्दो or चंदो; S. चन्द्रः
def: s.m. The moon; the crown of the head; a white spot in the forehead of cattle, &c. (and many other things which are moon-like); a semi-lunar gold ornament (worn under two others on the head); a target or mark (to shoot at); a lunar month
subs: s.m. Cloth figured with moons and stars; a robe made of flowered muslin, &c.
v.n. To change as the moon
v.n. To look out for, or to see, the new moon as soon as it is visible
s.f. The night of new moon, the first night of a lunar month
s.m.f. lit. 'Piece of the moon'; a lovely person (man or woman), a beauty (syn. mah-pāra)
v.n. The moon to rise (e.g. ćāṅd-ne khet kiyā, 'the moon rose, or has risen')
To fire at a target or mark
s.f. Firing at a target; target-practice
v.n. To have a splendid or beautiful appearance
be raised to a post of honour; — to make a display, to act or walk ostentatiously
s.m. The month of ẕī-qād (in which no festival is held, and which is considered unlucky).
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi چاند (cand /⁠cā̃d⁠/) (c. 1503), / چند (cnd /⁠cand⁠/) (c. 1435), from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀘𑀁𑀤 (caṃda), from Sanskrit चन्द्र (candrá). Doublet of چَنْدا (candā).

def: s.f. = ćāṅṛ, q.v.

ety: old P. ćant; Pehl. ćand; Zend. ćvant, rt. ću; S. चियत्
def: adj. How much? how many? how often? how long? — so many; several, some, a few, sundry; (in comp.) quantity, as much or many, times, fold (e.g. du-ćand, 'two-fold')
subs: adj. & adv. Several, various; exceeding, excessive; many times, many fold
adj. Of or for a few days, ephemeral, temporary, transitory, transient
har-ćand-ki, adj. Much as, with all that, so much the more; although, notwithstanding, nevertheless, however.

def: s.m. The moon ( = ćāṅd)
subs: ćand-mukh, s.m. A face as bright or as beautiful as the moon; — adj. = ćand-badanā, ćand-badanī, or ćand-mukhā, or ćand-mukhī, adj. Moon-faced, having a face as beautiful as the moon ( = S. ćandra-vadana, ćandra-mukha, &c.).

def: adj. (f. -ā), Fierce, violent, impetuous, hot, passionate, wrathful, furious; hot, pungent, acrid; — s.m. A violent person, &c.
s.m. = ćānda, q.v.
s.m. = ćāṅdā, q.v.
s.m. The moon ( = ćand, ćandra); — a round piece or slice
subs: adj. As bright and beautiful as the sun and moon.

def: s.f. A passionate or furious woman; a name of the goddess Durgā (applied especially to her incarnation for the purpose of destroying the Asur or demon Mahesh. This exploit forms the subject of a section of the Mārkaṇḍeya-purāna, and is particularly celebrated in Bengal at the Durgā-pūjā, or festival held in honour of this goddess in the month ʼAswin, Sept.-Oct.).
s.m. A common theodolite station of the Revenue Survey (forming the ends of the main lines from which the village boundaries are laid down).
s.m. Subscription, &c. ( = ćandā, q.v.).

ety: for ćanda, rel. n. fr. ćand, q.v.
def: s.m. Contribution, subscription, donation; assessment; — the fund for remounts (in police accounts)
subs: To pay a contribution, give a subscription or donation

def: s.m. = ćanḍāl, q.v.
s.m. contrac. of ćanḍāl, q.v.
s.m. The most degraded of the mixed tribes; a man of this tribe (born from a Śūdra father and a Brāhman mother); an outcast, a miscreant, a vile wretch, a malignant or diabolical creature; a miser
subs: s.m. A hair on the head (considered unlucky)
s.m. A company of four or more miscreants.

def: s.f. A woman of the ćanḍāl caste; the wife of a ćanḍāl; a degraded vile woman, &c.
s.f. A woman of the ćanḍāl caste; the wife of a ćanḍāl; a degraded vile woman, &c.
s.f. A woman of the ćanḍāl caste; the wife of a ćanḍāl; a degraded vile woman, &c.
s.f. A woman of the ćanḍāl caste; the wife of a ćanḍāl; a degraded vile woman, &c.
s.m. Name of a caste of sweepers.
s.m. A caste of sweepers; a member of that caste ( = ćandāliyā).
s.m. Name of a clan of Rājpūts.
s.m. The sandal tree, Santalum album, or Sirium myrtifolium; sandal-wood, sandal, sanders; the unctuous preparation of sandal-wood (held in high estimation as a perfume); — a proper name.
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit चन्दन (candana). Doublet of صَنْدَل (sandal).

def: adj. & adv. Much, many, numbers, more, as many as, so much, so many, so great, how much soever, how many soever; so greatly
subs: There is no great necessity, it is not very necessary
adv. As much as, as many as, as oft as, as soon as, as long as; whatever, so greatly; insomuch that; how much soever; notwithstanding that, although
It is of no great consequence; there is no great object; no matter.

def: s.f. The light half of a month.
adj. & s.m. Sundry; miscellaneous; — a variety of petty taxes formerly levied by the Mog̠al Government (such as those on musicians, showmen, &c.); sundries, miscellaneous charges, &c.
s.m. = ćandra-hār, q.v.s.v. ćandra.
s.m. = ćanḍāl, q.v.
v.n. = ćandrānā, q.v.
s.f. = S چنڐتو चण्डत्व ćanḍatva, s.m. Warmth, pungency; passionateness, fierceness, furiousness.
s.m. = S چنڐتا चण्डता ćanḍatā, s.f. Warmth, pungency; passionateness, fierceness, furiousness.
s.m. The rear-guard (of an army); — camp-followers.
s.m. = ćāndrāyaṇ, q.v.
adj. & adv. Some, somewhat, a few, a little, a while
subs: adv. After a while, in due course.

def: s.f. Name of a village on the left bank of the Betwā (remarkable for a fine species of cotton fabric manufactured there); — also = ćandelī, q.v.
s.m. Name of a tribe of Rājpūts who claim descent from the moon.

s.f. A very fine species of cotton cloth (made originally at Ćandel or Canderī. The thread of the best quality is sold for its weight in silver; and the cloth is of so costly a description as to be used in native courts only).
s.f. Name of a village on the left bank of the Betwā (remarkable for a fine species of cotton fabric manufactured there); — also = ćandelī, q.v.
s.m. A deep place.
s.f. Pure silver; plate; money; the crown of the head
subs: s.m. 'A silver shoe'; a bribe, a douceur
v.t. To make white; to burn to ashes
v.n. To be burnt to ashes.

def: s.f. = ćanḍā, q.v.
adj. (f. -ī), Silvery; white.
s.f. A name of Durgā ( = ćanḍā, q.v.).
s.m. Name of a tribe of Rājpūts who claim descent from the moon.

ety: ćand, q.v.+īn = Zend aēna = S. in
def: adj. So much, so many (see ćandāṅ).
adj. Silvery.
adj. & s.m. = ćandāna, q.v.
s.f. The crown of the head; — a round flat cake (of flour or meal).

ety: ćāṅd+Prk. अडआ = S. अ+र or ल+कः; or S. चन्द्रकः
def: s.m. A wafer-like ornament of gold or tinsel worn on the forehead; — the ceremony of putting the ornament on the foreheads of persons at the drawing up of a marriage contract, as the ratification of the agreement.
adj. Bald ( = ćandrā, q.v.); — s.m. Baldness.

ety: ćāṅd+Prk. अलओ = S. अ+र+कः; cf. S. चान्द्र+कः
def: s.m. Moonlight; light, splendour, brilliance; — a pigeon with white wings; a species of pheasant, Gallophasis albocristatus (common in the Himalayas)
subs: ćāṅdnā-pakh, s.m. The light fortnight of the moon
v.n. To be light, to dawn; to be lighted up; to have the sensation of lights or stars dancing before the eyes (from a blow, &c.; syn. teʼorānā); to be crushed by a misfortune.

def: s.m. Moonlight ( = ćāṅdnā); — epithet of a parrot.

ety: ćāṅd+Prk. अलिआ = S. अ+र+इका; or S. चन्द्रिका
def: s.f. Moonlight, moonbeam; — a kind of white flower, Tabernæmontana coronaria; — a white cloth spread over a carpet; an awning, a canopy or cope; anything white and shining
subs: s.m. An epithet of the moon
s.m. A wide and public street or market
v.n. The moonlight to be bright
s.m. The practice of Brahmans and others wounding themselves in order to extort alms or the payment of a debt
(-ko), To be moon-struck, to be paralyzed from a stroke of the moon (a horse).

def: s.m. contrac. of ćanḍāwal, q.v.
s.m. A sort of sedan ( = ćau-ḍol); — a plaything consisting of four little earthen pots joined together.
s.m. A small canopy, an awning; the crown (of a hat, &c.); — the scraper for getting the guṛ out of the boiler in a kolhū; — the shaft of a ploughshare.
adj. & s.m. Lunar; relating to the moon, regulated by the moon; — a lunar month; the light fortnight or half month during which the moon is on the increase; — the moon-gem
subs: s.f. ( = ćandra-bhāgā), The river Chenāb in the Panjāb
(fr. ćandra-mas), adj. & s.m. Relating or belonging to the moon, lunar; — name of the fifth lunar mansion, the constellation Mr̤iga-śiras; the stars in Orion.

def: adj. Bald ( = ćandlā); — wise, intelligent.
s.m. The moon; a moon-like spot
subs: (ćandra+ātapa), s.m. Moonlight; — an open hall, one only furnished with a roof; an awning
(ra˚+as˚), s.m. The setting (or the time of the setting) of the moon
s.m. Name of a mountain in the Himalaya in which the river Chenab has its source
s.f. The river Chenab (one of the five streams of the Panjāb)
s.m. Phase of the moon; new moon
s.m. A messenger from the moon (regarded as a deity)
s.m. The moon (regarded as a deity)
s.f. A digit of the moon ( = ćandr-lekhā)
s.m. lit. 'One who has the dust of the moon'; a plagiarist, a poetical thief
s.m. An epithet of kshīr-sāgar as the place whence the moon first came
s.m. (Astrol.) Death occasioned by the influence of the moon
adj. & s.m. Lovely as the moon; — the moon-stone, a fabulous gem (supposed to be formed from the congelation of the rays of the moon and to dissolve under the influence of its light)
s.f. The wife of the moon; the night
s.f. A digit or one-sixteenth of the moon's disc; a lunar crescent; — a kind of dhotī, or petticoat (worn by women)
s.m. 'Moon-protected'; the fabled registrar of Yama's court
s.f. A digit of the moon; — the plant Serratula anthelmintica; — a species of metre
s.m. The sphere or heaven of the moon
s.m. The orb or disc of the moon; the lunar sphere; a halo round the moon
adj. & s.m. 'Moon-crested'; — the moon-crested one, an epithet of Śiva
ćandr-vadanī, adj. Moon-faced, having a face as fair as the moon

vulg. ćandr-uday (ćandra+udaya), s.m. Rising of the moon, moon-rise; an awning, a cloth or sheet spread over the large open courts of Hindu houses upon festive occasions; — a mercurial preparation used in medicine; a kind of collyrium
s.f. The moon-plant, Asclepias acida; rue
vulg. ćandr-bimb, s.m. The disc of the moon
s.m. The nasal sign ँ (seldom used in Hindī, the anuswār being used instead)
s.m. The lunar race, a certain race or dynasty of Kshatriyas who claim descent from the moon
vulg. ćandr-bansī, adj. & s.m. Of or appertaining to the lunar race; — one of the lunar race or dynasty
s.m. A necklace composed of circular pieces of gold, silver, &c., or of white stone beads worked with gold, &c. (vulg. ćandan-hār)
s.m. lit. 'Deriding the moon'; a glittering scimitar.
wety: From Sanskrit चन्द्र (candrá). Doublet of چاند (cā̃d).

def: s.m. The eye in a peacock's tail; — a finger-nail.

ety: ćandra+ā = āw = Prk. आवे = S. आपि (caus. augment)+nā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.n. To be withered or blighted (a tree), to wither or dry up, to cease to grow.

ety: ćand˚ fr. S. छदस, rt. छद्+rā caus. augment
def: v.n. To make pretexts or excuses, to practise concealment, to pretend ignorance; to impute falsehood (to).
s.m. Gum anime, or gum copal.
s.m. See s.v. ćandra.
s.f. Acquaintance; companion; connexion.

s.m. A religious observance or expiatory penance regulated by the moon's age (it consists in diminishing the daily consumption of food by one mouthful every day for the dark half of the month, beginning with 15 at the full moon until the quantity is reduced to 0 at the new moon, and then increasing in like manner during the fortnight of the moon's increase).
s.f. Moonlight, moonbeam, moonshine; — a kind of fish, Zeus oblongus; — large or small cardamoms; — a species of metre.
s.m. A barber; — a name of Śiva; — the pot-herb Chenopodium album.
s.m. The moon; — the deity of the moon.
s.m. An intoxicating drug made of opium
subs: s.m. One addicted to smoking ćanḍū
s.m. A house in which ćanḍū is smoked.

def: s.m. The pyramid-crested lark, Galerita cristata.

ety: ćāṅd, q.v.+awā = Prk. अओ (with व inserted) = S. अ+कः
def: s.m. A covering, an awning; a cloth ceiling; a glass spangle, &c. worn on the forehead by women (cf. ćāṅdlā).
s.m. The shaft of a ploughshare ( = ćandwā).
s.m. A small canopy, an awning; the crown (of a hat, &c.); — the scraper for getting the guṛ out of the boiler in a kolhū; — the shaft of a ploughshare.
adj. Fierce, passionate, &c. ( = ćanḍ, q.v.).
adj. Fierce, passionate, &c. ( = ćanḍ, q.v.).
v.n. To be unsteady, be restless, &c.; see ćanćal.
s.f. Spices given to cattle, drugs for oxen.

s.m. 'Anything crooked or bent'; the hand expanded with the fingers somewhat hooked: claw (of a wild beast), talon (of a bird); a Jew's harp, a lute; a paper kite with small cymbals attached; a streamer or flutterer of paper fastened to a kite; a name of one of the eight suits at cards; — fame, report
subs: ćaṅg-bāyu, s.m. Rheumatic or other pain in the back or a limb (causing it to be bent or curved; cf. ćanak-bāʼo)
s.f. Playing on the harp or lute, &c.

def: adj. (f. -ī), Sound, good; true; pure; right, &c. ( = aććhā); well, in health, healthy; healed, cured, convalescent
subs: (-ko), To put to rights, to correct, punish, chastise
(-ko), To make well, to restore to health, to heal, to cure
v.n. To be healed, be cured, become well, &c.
adj. (f. -ī), Sound, good, perfect; in health and vigour, hale and hearty; uninjured; tolerable, passable, fair.

def: s.m. = ćaṅgul, q.v.
s.m. = ćaṅger, q.v.
s.m.(?) A herb which springs out of ruined buildings (the seed of which, called ḵẖubāzī, is used in medicine).
s.f. A small basket or bowl (for holding flowers, garlands, &c.), a flower-pot, a tray.
s.m. A large basket; a tray; a trough.
s.f. = ćaṅger, q.v.
s.m. One who plays on the ćaṅg or harp, &c., a harper.
adj. (f. -ī), Healthy, sound, robust; expert, active, smart
s.m. Claw (of a wild beast); talon (of a bird); hand; clutch, grasp; hook; — a handful or fist-full (of anything dry).

ety: Prk. चंगुल्लो; S. चङ्ग+उलः or ऊलः
def: adj. Excellent, sound; flourishing, prosperous.
s.f. A small species of chick-pea.
s.m. = ćāʼīṅ, q.v.
= H چنيال चनियाल ćaniyāl, s.m.(?) Land under a crop of ćanā.
= H چنيادا चनियादा ćaniyādā, चनेआदा ćaneʼādā, s.m.(?) Land under a crop of ćanā.

s.m. A stamp or mark (fixed on a stack or heap of grain, &c.; cf. ćāṅp; ćhāpā); — a ceremony observed in the threshing ground at the time of forming the winnowed corn into a heap (cf. baṛhāwan, and ćhattur).
s.m. corr. of śankh, and of ćang, qq.v.

r: ćankā, ćanak, ćinak, ćanaknā, ćik, ćanak-bāʼo
def: s.m. = ćanak-bāʼī, s.f. Rheumatic pain, rheumatism (in the back, &c.).

s.m. A stamp or mark (fixed on a stack or heap of grain, &c.; cf. ćāṅp; ćhāpā); — a ceremony observed in the threshing ground at the time of forming the winnowed corn into a heap (cf. baṛhāwan, and ćhattur).
s.f. (dialec.) = ćākī, ćakkī, q.v.

ety: see ćāṅk; and cf. ćapkānā, and ćāṅpnā
def: v.t. To stamp or mark.
s.m. (dialec.) = ćānnā, ćāṅdnā, q.v.

s.f. = H چاننا चान्ना ćānnā, s.m. = ćāṅdnā, and ćāṅdnī, qq.v.
s.f. A bow; the lock of a gun; the stocks; the rack
subs: (-kī), To bend a bow; to cock a gun; — to punish or torture by squeezing the ear under the lock of a gun; to put in the stocks.

def: s.f. The tree Michelia kisopa (cf. ćampā).
s.m. Kneaded flour, dough.
s.f. (dialec.), A key ( = ćābī, q.v.).

ety: ćāṅp˚ = ćāp˚ = Prk. चंपे(इ) = S. चपय(ति), rt. चप्
def: v.t. To press, squeeze, to knead (dough, or the limbs), to shampoo; to join; to thrust in, stuff, cram.
s.f. A post, pillar; a prop, support; a lever ( = ḍheṅklī).
s.m. Cress, cresses, Lepidium sativum; syn. hālim.
s.m. A miser (cf. ćunćuṭā).

ety: rt. of ćāṅṭnā+Prk. अओ = S. अ+कः
def: s.m. A slap, a thump, a cuff.

ety: rt. of ćāṅṭnā+ī = Prk. एअव्वअइआ = S. (इ)तव्य+ता+इका
def: s.f. Cesses levied from (lit. squeezed out of) artisans, &c.

ety: ćāṅṭ˚ = Prk. चाडे(इ) = S. चाटय(ति), caus. of rt. चट्
def: v.t. To press, squeeze ( = ćāṅpnā); to strike with the flat of the hand, to slap, to thump ( = ćaṭiyānā).
s.m. Uncooked rice ( = ćāwal, q.v.)
s.m. A small species of millet.
s.m. The Bos grunniens, &c. ( = ćamar, q.v.); — the male flower of maize
subs: To shake a ćaṅwar or whisk (for the purpose of driving away flies, &c.), to whisk off flies with a ćaurī.

def: s.m. Uncooked rice ( = ćāwal, q.v.)
s.m. A bow (syn. dhanush; kamān); are of a circle
subs: adj. Of the form of a bow, bow-shaped; arched
s.m. The chord of an arc

def: adj. & s.m. Left (hand or side); discordant, inharmonious; unlucky, sinister; — the left hand, the left side
subs: To run zigzag when pursued by an enemy (in order to baffle him), to dodge
adj., adv. & s.m. Right and left; — carelessness; unsteadiness, want of principle.

def: s.m. The refuse of the jhaṛ-berī after the pālā is beaten from it
subs: s.m. Gross measurement of the lands of an estate.

def: s.f. = ćabak, ṭapak, qq.v.

ety: ćapak˚ = Prk. चपक्क(इ) or चपक्के(इ), fr. S. चप or चर्प+कृ
def: v.n. To be compressed; to be pressed flat down or on; to collapse; to stick, adhere; to cling (to); — to take up (with), form an unlawful connexion (with), to be entangled (in an amour), to fall in love (with); to spread or sink (as ink on damp paper)
subs: v.n. (intens.) To stick close (to), to stick together; to be compressed, &c. = ćipaknā; — to be sold on account of (its) sweetness (cf. pićaknā, and ćipaṭnā).

def: s.m. Fickleness, unsteadiness; mobility, quick movement, quickness, swiftness.

adj. & s.m. (f. -ā) & adv. Shaking, trembling, tremulous, unsteady, agitated, restless, wavering; variable, inconstant, fickle, uncertain, volatile, giddy, wanton; active, quick, swift, agile, expeditious; swiftly, quickly, &c.; — an unsteady person, &c.
subs: adj. Of a fickle or inconstant nature
wety: Borrowed from Telugu చెప్పులు (ceppulu), the plural of చెప్పు (ceppu).

def: adj. Foul, filthy, nastly, disgusting, brutal, beastly.
wety: Borrowed from Telugu చెప్పులు (ceppulu), the plural of చెప్పు (ceppu).

def: s.f. Lightning; the goddess Lakshmī or Fortune; a wanton, a harlot.

r: ćapalāʼī, ćapal-pan, ćapalatā, ćapalatva, ćapal, ćapalatā karnā
def: s.f. = H چپلپن चपलपन ćapal-pan, s.m. = S چپلتا चपलता ćapalatā, s.f. = S چپلتو चपलत्व ćapalatva, s.m. Activity, briskness, quickness, nimbleness, agility; unsteadiness, inconstancy, fickleness, giddiness, frivolity, levity, wantonness, &c. (see ćapal)

r: ćapalatā, ćapalāʼī, ćapal-pan, ćapalatva, ćapal, ćapalatā karnā
def: s.f. = H چپلائي चपलाई ćapalāʼī, s.f. = H چپلپن चपलपन ćapal-pan, s.m. = S چپلتو चपलत्व ćapalatva, s.m. Activity, briskness, quickness, nimbleness, agility; unsteadiness, inconstancy, fickleness, giddiness, frivolity, levity, wantonness, &c. (see ćapal)

r: ćapalatva, ćapalāʼī, ćapal-pan, ćapalatā, ćapal, ćapalatā karnā
def: s.m. = H چپلائي चपलाई ćapalāʼī, s.f. = H چپلپن चपलपन ćapal-pan, s.m. = S چپلتا चपलता ćapalatā, s.f. Activity, briskness, quickness, nimbleness, agility; unsteadiness, inconstancy, fickleness, giddiness, frivolity, levity, wantonness, &c. (see ćapal)

r: ćapal-pan, ćapalāʼī, ćapalatā, ćapalatva, ćapal, ćapalatā karnā
def: s.m. = H چپلائي चपलाई ćapalāʼī, s.f. = S چپلتا चपलता ćapalatā, s.f. = S چپلتو चपलत्व ćapalatva, s.m. Activity, briskness, quickness, nimbleness, agility; unsteadiness, inconstancy, fickleness, giddiness, frivolity, levity, wantonness, &c. (see ćapal)
s.m. = ćāpal, q.v.
v.t. To cause (one) to feel shame, to abash; — to place one thing on another, to heap, pile; to press down, compress, flatten, squeeze, crush.
s.m. A chopper, an instrument for chopping.

ety: rt. of ćāpnā+aṛ = Prk. अडी = S. अ+री
def: s.f. Hard crusty soil, stony soil; a flat expanse of land, a plain (cf. next).

ety: S. चर्व+र, by redupl., cf. ćabhaṛ-ćabhaṛ; or onomat.
def: s.f. The sound made in chewing or eating; the sound of lapping; — adv. (Eating or chewing) with a noise; with the noise of lapping.
adj. & s.m. Low, mean; — a flatterer, a sycophant, a toady.
v.n. = ćapaṛnā, q.v.
v.n. To flee, run away, slip away (from, -se), to escape; to evade, elude; to shuffle; to desist, deny.

ety: rt. of ćāpnā+aṭ = Prk. अडी = S. अ+टी
def: s.f. Husk; bran ( = capṛā).

ety: S. चपटः or चर्पटः, through P. ćapćt
def: s.m. A slap, a thump, cuff, box
subs: s.f. 'The slapping place,' the cheek; the crown of the head; the head.

def: s.f. = ćapṭī (used in comp.)
s.f. (local) A shoe of the European fashion (having a heel-piece, in contradistinction to the Persian shoe).
s.f. = ćapātī, q.v.
s.f. A thin cake of unleavened bread.
s.f. dim. of ćapṭā, q.v.
v.n. To be flattened, to be pressed flat or even, become flat, &c.
v.t. To stick (a thing on), &c. = ćipkānā; to force (a thing, on a person).
s.m. The palm of the hand with the fingers extended; — s.f. A slap; a sudden misfortune, &c. ( = ćapeṭā).

s.m. The palm with the fingers extended; a slap, thump, cuff; a stroke, shock, a sudden misfortune; blast or influence (of an evil spirit); risk, loss, injury (cf. jhapaṭṭā; jhapeṭ); — adj. & s.m. Taken by violence, snatched; illegitimate; — an illegitimate son, a bastard.
s.f. A piece of ground, or a place, surrounded with marshes and mire.
s.m. A kind of fishing-net.
s.m. A bow-man, an archer.
s.f. A fight (with swords); an altercation, a row
s.f. A kind of (close-fitting) vest or short coat
subs: s.m. A vest like a ćapkan.

def: s.m. A flatterer, wheedler, a sycopnant.
s.f. Flattery, wheedling; fawning, sycophancy.
v.t. = ćāṅpnā, q.v.

ety: ćap˚ = Prk. चप्प(इ) = S. चप्य(ते), pass. of rt. चप्
def: v.n. To be compressed, be flattened; to be crushed or squeezed; — to be abashed or ashamed; to be bashful; to blush; to lower the eyes, to cower, quail; to submit, stoop; to be put to silence.
s.f. A flat lid or cover (of a cooking pot; syn. sar-posh; cf. ḍhaknī); a saucer; — the cap of the knee, knee-pan; — the crown of the head
subs: (or pānī leke) ḍub-marnā, 'To drown oneself in a saucer-full of water'; to be greatly abashed
'To lick the pot-lid'; to be contented with little.

ety: rt. of ćap-nā+S. पत्रिका prob. oṭī is a diminut. suff.
def: s.f. An old worn-out turban or cap; a flattened hat or cap.
s.m. A hand-breadth, a measure of four fingers, a span (of land)
subs: adv. Every span of land, every inch of ground.

def: s.f. A sandal; a slipper.
wety: Borrowed from Telugu చెప్పులు (ceppulu), the plural of చెప్పు (ceppu).

def: s.f. (dialec.), The prickly pear, Cactus indicus (syn. ṅāg-phanī).
s.m. A (large) lid or cover of a pot ( = ćapnī).
s.f. Pressing or kneading the limbs, shampooing.
s.m. A paddle, an oar; — (local), a sandal, a shoe with a wooden sole (syn. ćappal)
s.m. = ćāpṛā, q.v.
s.m. A cake of cow-dung ( = ćapṛī).
adv. Tenaciously, dog, gedly, persistently; suddenly, unexpectedly, all at once.
s.m. Shell lac, lac; husk, bran; clear land; — two pice (in the language of brokers).
v.t. = ćipṭānā, q.v.
v.t. = ćapṛānā, q.v.
v.t. To assert falsely, to falsify, to belie; to brazen.
s.f. = H چپڙاسي चपड़ासी ćapṛāsī, s.m. See ćaprās, and ćaprāsī.

ety: said to be contrac. fr. P. ćaprāst; but cf. ćapṭā
def: s.f. A breast-plate, a plate worn on a belt (as a mark of office), a badge; a clasp, a buckle.
s.m. One who wears a ćaprās or badge, a peon, orderly, beadle, messenger (of a court, &c.)
s.m. = H چپڙاس चपड़ास ćapṛās, s.f. See ćaprās, and ćaprāsī.
adj. Brazen-faced, shameless, bold, impudent; — s.m. A slipper.
adv. Quickly, instantly, forthwith.
s.f. A puddle; — a small species of pulse (resembling ćanā); — husk, bran (cf. ćapṛā).
s.f. A cake of cow-dung.

ety: S. पिच्चट or पिच्चिट+कः, rt. पिच्च्; or per met. चिपट or चिपिट+कः; cf.ćapnā
def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Pressed flat, flattened, compressed; flat, even, level; shallow and broad (as a vessel); — a person with a flat nose; — a large flat earthen vessel

ety: ćapṭā+Prk. आव or आवे = S. आपि caus. augment+nā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.t. To press flat, to flatten, to press even.
adj. = ćapṭā, q.v.
s.f. Congressus libidinosus duarum mulierum ( = ćapaṭ-bāzī)
subs: ćapṭī laṛānā, ćapṭī laṛnā, Libidini sapphicæ indulgere

def: s.f. A kind of insect.

ety: Prk. चत्तारि = S.चत्वारि, neut. pl. of चतुर्; — the Pers. ćār is a contrac. of ćahār, q.v.
def: adj. Four
subs: s.m. A kind of dervish who shaves his eye-brows and whiskers
s.m. Shaving of the eye-brows and whiskers; shaving of the head, eye-brows, moustache, and beard
s.m. 'Four people'; people (in general); a jury of arbitrators
s.f. 'Four eyes;' the meeting of the eyes of two people; meeting, interview ( = ćār ćashm)
s.m. The breadth of four fingers; a measure equal to that breadth; a palm; a gira, or two and a half inches
s.m. A kind of armour (it is so called from four plates of steel which are fixed on the breast)
s.m. A kind of large cushion (on which the great sit or recline); a throne; a couch, sofa
adj. Divided into four, four-pieced, four square, in four parts
s.f. A bedstead, a bed; a litter for carrying the sick; — ćār-pāʼī-par paṛnā, v.n. To take to (one's) bed; to become sick, be confined to (one's) bed
s.m. A crossing of two roads, a quadrivium
s.m. The four feet of religion, viz. truth, purity, beneficence, and mercy
ćār-tāra, s.m. lit. 'Having four strings'; a lute with four strings
s.m. A mixture of four kinds of seeds (tuḵẖm-ě-raiḥān, isbag̠ol, bālanga, and ḵẖurfa) used as a cure for dysentery
s.f. Full gallop; — adv. At full gallop
s.m. A kind of saddle made of cloth (lit. of four folds) and without a tree
s.m. The going (and returning) of the bride and bridegroom to their respective homes four times in the month after marriage
s.f. = ćār āṅkheṅ, q.v.; — adj. Faithless, inconstant ( = totā-ćashm)
adj. Fourfold, four times (as much), quadruple
adj. & s.m. Chequered, checked; — chequered cloth; checked muslin; cloth ornamented with squares; tripe (of a ruminant)
s.m. lit. 'Four teeth'; a four-year old ( = ćār-sāl)
s.m. The four quarters of the globe
s.m. lit. 'Four days,' a few days (e.g. ćār din-kī zindagī hai)
adj. For a few days, temporary, fleeting, transient
s.f. A courtyard, enclosure, area; a wall round a town, ramparts; — ćār-dīhāt, s.m. An estate formed of the land belonging to four villages
s.m. A mode of sitting (like that of a tailor at work); — ćār-zānū baiṭhnā, v.n. To squat, to sit cross-legged
s.m. A cross-road, two roads crossing each other; a square or court; — adv. On all sides, all round
adv. On all sides, all round, everywhere
s.m. An article of dress so called, a kind of vest; — a kind of mirror; — ćār kāg̠aẕ, s.m. The proceedings in a law-suit, viz. the plaint, defence, replication, and rejoinder
s.f. A square, a quadrangle; — ćār-konī-kī ćārolī, s.f. The kernel of the Chironjia sapida or Buchanania latifolia
s.m. The four quarters of the globe, the whole world ( = ćār-dāṅg)
adj. Fourfold, four times; — ćār-mag̠z, s.m. A walnut; — an earthen ball with which children play
adj. The four, the whole four, all four
s.m. The four religious orders among Hindūs
s.m. The four chief things, viz. arth, dharm, kām, and moksh
s.m. The four yugas or epochs of the world, viz. the Satya or Krita, Treta, Dwāpar, and Kālī
s.f. The four states of man; the four states of a student of the Vedānta philosophy, viz. waking, sleeping, unconsciousness, and absorption into the one universal spirit of Brahma
adv. On all sides, all round, everywhere
adv. At full length, on the back, sprawling
adv. = ćāroṅ diśā, ćāroṅ or, qq.v.
Four verses of the Qǒrʼān (used as a charm)
adv. On all four sides, all around, in all directions
s.m. The four chief castes among the Hindūs
s.m. The four successors of Mohammad, viz. Abū-bakr, Omar, Osmān, and ʻAlī
s.m. A sect of Musalmāns who venerate equally the four successors of Mohammad (the term is applied by the Shīʻas to the Sunnīs)
s.m. A square silver coin (used as a charm to discover a thief)
ćār-ek, s.m. One part out of four, a fourth part.
wety: Inherited from Old Hindi चारि (cāri), from Prakrit *𑀘𑀬𑀸𑀭𑀺 (*cayāri), from Sanskrit चत्वारि (catvā́ri), चत्वारः (catvā́raḥ). More information Compare the attested Prakrit 𑀘𑀢𑁆𑀢𑀭𑀺 (cattari). The alteration was probably based off the Sanskrit accusative चतुरः (caturaḥ) without a consonant cluster where intervocalic t > y is expected, but could also be an abnormal numeral phonological development.

def: s.m. A present on a festive occasion.
wety: Inherited from Old Hindi चारि (cāri), from Prakrit *𑀘𑀬𑀸𑀭𑀺 (*cayāri), from Sanskrit चत्वारि (catvā́ri), चत्वारः (catvā́raḥ). More information Compare the attested Prakrit 𑀘𑀢𑁆𑀢𑀭𑀺 (cattari). The alteration was probably based off the Sanskrit accusative चतुरः (caturaḥ) without a consonant cluster where intervocalic t > y is expected, but could also be an abnormal numeral phonological development.

def: s.m. A spy, scout, secret agent or emissary; — the tree Chironjia sapida or Buchanania latifolīa.
wety: Inherited from Old Hindi चारि (cāri), from Prakrit *𑀘𑀬𑀸𑀭𑀺 (*cayāri), from Sanskrit चत्वारि (catvā́ri), चत्वारः (catvā́raḥ). More information Compare the attested Prakrit 𑀘𑀢𑁆𑀢𑀭𑀺 (cattari). The alteration was probably based off the Sanskrit accusative चतुरः (caturaḥ) without a consonant cluster where intervocalic t > y is expected, but could also be an abnormal numeral phonological development.

def: s.m. (contrac. of ćāra), s.m. Remedy, &c. (see ćāra)
subs: ćār-o-nā-ćār, adv. Certainly, inevitably, perforce, right or wrong, nolens volens.
wety: Inherited from Old Hindi चारि (cāri), from Prakrit *𑀘𑀬𑀸𑀭𑀺 (*cayāri), from Sanskrit चत्वारि (catvā́ri), चत्वारः (catvā́raḥ). More information Compare the attested Prakrit 𑀘𑀢𑁆𑀢𑀭𑀺 (cattari). The alteration was probably based off the Sanskrit accusative चतुरः (caturaḥ) without a consonant cluster where intervocalic t > y is expected, but could also be an abnormal numeral phonological development.

ety: Prk. चडिआ; S. चटित्वा, rt. चट्
def: s.f. A turf ( = caktā); — a scar, mark, wound, hurt, knock ( = ćoṭ); — a lever ( = ćāṅṛ).

adj. & s.m. Moving, going, shifting (as sand, &c.); movable, locomotive; animate; following, practising (commonly used at the end of comp.); — a spy, a secret emissary or agent; — a wagtail; — pasture, forage; food; — a sandbank, an island formed by the current of a river, a shoal, a ford (cf. ćarh)
subs: (ćara+aćara), vulg. ćar-aćar, adj. & s.m. Movable and immovable, locomotive and stationary, animate and inanimate; — the aggregate of all created things, animate and inanimate things; the world; the sky; atmosphere; heaven
adj. & s.m. (dialec.) Grazing; graminivorous; — an animal that grazes ( = ćarand).

def: s.m.(f.?), The sound made by a breaking branch, or in tearing cloth; sound of cracking or tearing
subs: ćaṛ-ā-ćaṛ, s.m.(f.?) = ćaṛ (with the addition of the idea of repetition, or continuity).

def: s.m.(f.?) Sound made by tearing cloth, &c. ( = ćaṛ, q.v.).

ety: Prk. चरेअव्वअं; S. चरितव्य+कं, rt. चर्
def: s.m. Food (for cattle), fodder, forage, herbage; provisions; bait (for fish); a young plant
s.m. Remedy, &c. = P. ćāra, q.v.
s.m. = چارا ćārā, q.v.
s.m. Grazing, pasturing; pasture
subs: s.f. Grazing-ground; grazing-land, pasture; a meadow.

ety: akin to S. चर्; cf. H. ćārā
def: adj. Admitting of remedy, remedi able
subs: s.m. One who remedies or cures
s.f. Preparing a remedy; using means for a remedy, remedying, curing; cure; — ćāra-gar, s.m. = ćāra-sāz, q.v.

def: v.n. (local) = ćarab honā, q.v.s.v. ćarb.
adj. & s.m. See s.v. ćar.
v.t. To perfume (the body) by smearing or plastering it with an unguent made of sandal-wood, to anoint; to worship.
s.m. A lamp; a light
subs: s.f. A wick for a lamp; a lamp and a wick; lights
s.m. Time for lighting lamps, dark, evening
To prepare the lamps for lighting
ćirāg̠ baṛhānā, To extinguish a lamp, put out a light
adj. Rearing (a horse); — ćirāg̠-pā honā, v.n. To rear (as a horse); to be enraged or angry
To extinguish a lamp ( = ćirāg̠ bujhānā)
v.n. A lamp to be lighted; a lamp to burn
v.n. A lamp to be lighted from another; (fig.) to derive profit one from another
ćirāg̠ gul karnā, To put out a light, &c. ( = ćirāg̠ bujhānā)
v.n. A lamp to be extinguished
v.n. A lamp to be lighted; to be growing dark, night to set in
v.n. A light to drop sparks ( = phūl jharnā ćirāg̠-kā, — it is regarded as a good omen)
adj. Without a lamp or light, dark; cheerless, desolate; childless; — be-ćirāg̠ karnā, v.t. To render dark, to make cheerless or desolate; to ruin
v.n. 'Lamps to burn with butter in them' (in place of oil); to live in fine style, to make great rejoicings.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian چراغ (čirâġ, čarâġ).

def: s.f. See s.v. ćarā.
s.m. pl. (of ćirāg̠), Lamps; lights; a display of lamps, a general illumination.

s.f. Visiting the tomb of a saint, and depositing a votive lamp and an offering of money, &c. thereon; a present to the Mullā for offering oblations and a votive lamp at the tomb of a saint; alms given to beggars who walk the streets at night (as the class called naqsh-bandī); a present to soothsayers.
v.n. (local) = ćuragnā, curaknā, q.v.
s.f. ending out cattle to graze; grazing, pasturage; — grazing-ground, grass-land ( = ćarāʼo); the price paid for grazing; rent derived from pasturage.
s.m. = ćarwāhā, q.v.
s.m. A spy, a secret agent ( = ćār, q.v.).
s.m. An animal that grazes.
s.m. A spy, a secret emissary or agent; — name of a Muni and physician; name of a treatise on medicine (composed by Ćarak).

ety: S. चित्रकं; or contrac. of चर्म+चित्रकं
def: s.m. Leprosy.
adj. Last, hinder, ultimate, final; outermost; western, west
subs: (ćarama+aćala), ćarama-kshmābhr̤it, s.m. The western mountain behind which the sun and moon are supposed to set.

def: s.m. A wanderer; a pilgrim; a wandering actor or singer; a dancer; a mime; a bard.
adj. (dialec.) = ćāroṅ, q.v.s.v. ćār, 'four'; — s.m. = ćālan, q.v.
part. adj. Grazing (a beast).

s.m. The foot; base or root (of a tree, &c.); foot (of a verse), a dactyl; a single line (of a verse); a foot-soldier; a shoe; a sect, a school; — acting, dealing, conduct, practice, performance; good or moral conduct; fixed or instituted observance (for any class, age, or condition); — consuming, eating, feeding, grazing
subs: (ćārana+ābharaṇa), s.m. An ornament for the foot
(˚na+ar˚), s.m. The lotus-like foot (of a deity, lover, &c.)
(˚na+am˚), s.m. The dust of the feet

(˚na+am˚), s.m. 'Foot-nectar'; the water in which the feet of an idol, or a priest, or a Brahman, or a guru, or a husband, or a guest, have been washed; — ćaraṇāmr̤it lenā (-kā), To sip the water in which the feet of an idol, &c. have been washed
s.m. One who carries his master's shoes, shoe-bearer
part. Fallen at the feet, prostrate
s.m. Falling at the feet, prostration
s.m. A wooden shoe; a footstool
s.m. Lotus-foot; beautiful foot (as of a deity, a lover, &c.)
(-ke), To touch the feet (of a priest, &c.)

def: s.m. Lea fallow, a meadow, a plain; a salt marsh or meadow by the sea-shore.
v.t. (dialec.) = ćaṛhānā, q.v.
v.t. To tend grazing cattle, to graze, pasture; to feed; — to have (one) under command; to lead (one by the nose); to make a fool of.

ety: imperf. part. of ćarīdan, rt. Zend ćar = S. चर्
def: adj. & s.m. Grazing (an animal); graminivorous; — an animal that grazes, a beast, a quadruped

ety: imperf. part. of ćarīdan, rt. Zend ćar = S. चर्
def: adj. & s.m. Grazing (an animal); graminivorous; — an animal that grazes, a beast, a quadruped
s.m. (dialec.) = ćanḍūl, q.v.
s.m. (dialec.) = ćanḍūl, q.v.
s.f. A four-anna piece ( = ćau-annī).
s.m. Pasture-ground, a field or meadow fit for grazing.
s.m. (dialec.) = ćaṛhāʼo, q.v.
adj. = ćarpharā.
s.m. = ćaṛ, q.v.; — ćaṛaṛ-ćaṛaṛ, s.m. = ćaṛ-ćaṛ, q.v.
s.m. = ćaṛ, q.v.; — ćaṛaṛ-ćaṛaṛ, s.m. = ćaṛ-ćaṛ, q.v.
s.m.(f.?) = ćar-ćar, q.v.

s.m. A large leathern bag or bucket (for drawing water from wells; — syn. purwaṭ; moṭh); — the exudation of the flowers of hemp collected with the dew and prepared for use as an intoxicating drug (it is collected, it is said, by persons running through a field of gānjā, with leathern aprons)
s.m. = ćarsī, q.v.
s.m. The man who receives and empties the ćaras or leathern bag at the well-mouth.
s.m. A wagtail ( = ćar; syn. mamolā).
s.m. (dialec.) = ćaṛhāwā, q.v.
s.f. Tending cattle while grazing, cattle-feeding, grazing, pasturing.
s.f. Tending cattle while grazing, cattle-feeding, grazing, pasturing.

ety: imperf. part. of ćarīdan; see ćarān
def: adj. & s.m. Grazing; — a grazier.
adj. Fat, greasy, oily; viscous; thick, gross, grumous; smooth, glib; sharp, smart, quick, ready; — overpowering, prevailing; — s.m. An overmatch
subs: adj. & s.m. Having a nice hand at work; active, expert, dexterous, ingenious; — one who has a nice hand at work, an expert, &c.; (met.) a thief
adj. & s.m. Smooth-tongued, glib-tongued, fair-spoken, plausible; flattering; agreeable, entertaining, conversible; voluble, eloquent; — one who has a smooth tongue to persuade, wheedle, or deceive
s.f. Glibness of tongue, speciousness of language, plausibility, fairness of speech, fine talk; persuasion, flattery; volubility, eloquence; swagger
v.t. To anoint, to smear or rub over with oil or ghī; to broil in ghī or butter; to make sleek or supple
v.n. To be more than a match (for), to get the better (of), to prevail (against); to be sharper or more skilful (than).

def: s.m. = ćarba, q.v.
s.m. Thin vellum or paper used for tracing, tracing-paper; a copy (of a drawing, &c.) made by means of tracing-paper, a tracing
subs: (-kā), To make a tracing or copy (of), to trace.

ety: S. चर्व+आपि+अकः (i.e. आपकः); cf. ćābnā, ćabānā and ćarbarānā
def: adj. Smooth, spoken, plausible; smart (in conversation), smart, sharp, clever; — bold, brazen, impudent, shameless (cf. ćarbarāk; ćarpharā).
v.t. = ćarb karnā, q.v.s.v. ćarb.

ety: S. चर्म+ā = āw = Prk. आवे = S. आपि caus, augment
def: v.t. To brace (a drum).
adj. = ćarbāk, q.v.
s.f. Prating, chattering ( = baṛ-baṛ)
subs: ćaṛbaṛ karnā, v.n. To prate, chatter, jabber, gabble.

def: adj. a dial. var. of ćaṭ-patā, and ćarpharā, qq.v.; and cf. next.
s.f. Smoothness of speech, glibness of tongue, plausibility
s.m. Smart (in conversation), glib-tongued, plausible; fluent, voluble; — a glib-tongued person; a great talker (cf. ćarparā).
v.n. To speak smoothly; to speak or act plausibly; to be fluent or voluble.
adj. & s.m. Prating, talkative; prater, chatterer; gossip.
s.f. Fat, grease, suet, tallow
subs: adj. Fat, sleek; fatty, greasy; — ćarbī-kī jālī, s.f. The fat caul that covers the bowels, the omentum
s.f. The fine membrane that covers the fat; caul.

def: v.n. To form fat, to grow fat or sleek.
s.f. Covering or cleansing the body with unguents, smearing with ointment, anointing, smearing, plastering.

s.m. Repetition, recitation, careful perusal, consideration, deliberation, reflection; investigation, inquiry; discussion, argument; attention (to business), engagedness; prevalence; talking over past events, mentioning, incidental mention, discourse, popular talk, report, rumour, gossip; — practising, applying, cultivating (science, &c.); adoring
subs: (ćarćā+āvali), s.f. Discourse, mention, recapitulation; story, history, account
(-kā), To make mention (of), to talk or discourse (of or about); to report, to give out; — ćarće-meṅ rahnā (-ke), To continue engaged in, to apply oneself constantly to, &c.
